proofs.control: Data migrations are versioned and reversible
proofs.notes: 68 migrations, all version-prefixed (YYYYMMDDNNNN). Version uniqueness + SQL quality checked by pnpm verify:commands.

proofs.control: Referential integrity is enforced or explicitly handled

evidence.enforcement: PostgreSQL foreign key constraints in Supabase

proofs.notes: 74 foreign key declarations in generated types. PostgreSQL FK constraints enforce referential integrity. RLS enabled on canonical tables.

proofs.control: All critical tables have clear ownership and purpose
proofs.notes: 35 tables defined with 36 explicit owner assignments in canonicalSchema. TypeScript type enforcement.

evidence.audit_log_table: audit_log with entity_type, entity_id, entity_ref, action, actor, payload, created_at
evidence.lifecycle_events: pick_lifecycle table records every state transition
evidence.settlement_history: settlement_records with corrects_id — full correction chain

proofs.notes: Multiple audit surfaces: audit_log (all actions), pick_lifecycle (state transitions), submission_events, settlement_records (correction chain), promotion_history. Full historical traceability.

proofs.control: No silent data mutation occurs outside defined flows

evidence.single_writer: apps/api is the only canonical DB writer (CLAUDE.md invariant)
evidence.repository_pattern: All writes go through RepositoryBundle — no direct DB client creation in services
evidence.writer_authority: packages/db/src/writer-authority.ts — 5 registered fields with explicit write authorization
evidence.rls: Row-level security enabled on canonical tables (UTV2-604)
evidence.audit_trail: All critical writes produce audit_log entries with actor attribution

proofs.notes: Single writer (API), repository pattern, writer authority (5 fields), RLS on canonical tables, audit trail on all writes. No silent mutation path.
